content.problemStatement:
Toofan is playing an exciting game where there are NNN rings, and for each ring, Toofan can either SWISH or PASS. The goal of the game is to achieve **at least** KKK swishes.

The game will end **immediately** when at least one of the following two conditions is met:


Toofan has finished all the NNN rings; or
It it impossible to win the game any more.

This means it is impossible for Toofan to get at least KKK swishes, regardless of what he does with the remaining rings.
Toofan knows the value of KKK (the number of swishes he must achieve), and remembers the sequence of moves he made. However, he no longer remembers the value of NNN (the total number of rings).

You are given a string SSS of length MMM, denoting the moves Toofan made during the game. Each character of SSS is either `S` (denoting a swish) or `P` (denoting a pass).

Given that Toofan ended the game after **exactly** this sequence of MMM moves, what could the value of NNN be?


Input Format

The first line of input contains a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line contains two space-separated integers MMM and KKK — the number of moves made by Toofan, and the number of swishes he needs to achieve to win.
The second line contains a string SSS of length MMM, describing the game played by Toofan.




Output Format
For each testcase, output a single integer — the number of rings NNN in the game.
constraints: Each character of SSS is either `'S'` or `'P'`.
The sum of MMM across all tests won't exceed 2⋅1052\cdot 10^52⋅105.
It is guaranteed only 1 such value of answer - N exists.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: Toofan has made one swish and two passes so far, and needs to achieve 444 swishes.
If N=5N = 5N=5

First move: Swish (It's still possible to win, so game continues)
Second move: Pass (It's still possible to win, so game continues)
Third move: Pass (It's impossible to win, so game ends, as you can't get 3 more swishes in just 2 rings left)

It can be proved that only N = 555 satisfies this condition.
Test case 222: K=1K = 1K=1, and Toofan already had 222 swishes when the game ended. This means he must've finished all NNN rings and won the game, so the answer is simply the number of moves he made, i.e. 333.
